name: diagnosing-bugs
description: Symptom-driven diagnosis loop for hard bugs and performance regressions — narrows down via observation + experiment rather than guessing. Form ≥3 hypotheses about the cause, predict the distinguishing observation for each, make the smallest observation that falsifies one. Pairs with debugging-and-error-recovery (the protocol: reproduce / localize / reduce / fix / guard) by adding the diagnostic discipline inside the localize-and-reduce phases. Core skill because hard bugs are a guaranteed part of every project; the discipline is more universal than the specifics.

positive_triggers.input: Checkout endpoint is slow for 1% of requests — no clear cause. Walking through the localize step in debugging-and-error-recovery but the obvious causes don't pan out.
positive_triggers.reason: Localize step in progress + obvious causes ruled out → matches Use when 'debugging-and-error-recovery's Step 2 (Localize) is in progress and the bug's cause is non-obvious'

positive_triggers.input: Latency regression on the auth service since Tuesday; can't tell if it's a DB issue, dependency issue, or traffic-pattern issue. Multiple observations attempted, no convergence.
positive_triggers.reason: Performance regression with no clear cause → matches Use when 'performance regression that doesn't match a known failure mode'

positive_triggers.input: We've tried 'maybe it's the cache', 'maybe it's the connection pool', 'maybe it's the new deploy' — none of them fit. Need a structured hypothesis-driven approach.
positive_triggers.reason: Tried multiple hypotheses without convergence → matches Use when 'tried 1-2 hypotheses and they didn't pan out'

negative_triggers.input: Bug with obvious cause: missing import, typo in env var, syntax error. Just fix it.
negative_triggers.reason: Bug with obvious cause → matches NOT for 'bug with obvious cause' → fix directly without skill invocation

negative_triggers.input: Production alert fired, error rate 5x baseline — what's the runbook?
negative_triggers.reason: Active in-flight incident → matches NOT for 'production incident in-flight' → production-incident-playbook routes here; come back to diagnosing-bugs at Phase 4 if deeper investigation needed

negative_triggers.input: Production p99 regressed to 1s — find the bottleneck.
negative_triggers.reason: Post-hoc performance optimization with no clear repro → matches NOT for 'performance regression hunting post-deploy (no clear repro)' → performance-optimization routes here

behavioral_evals.scenario: Engineer reaches for diagnosing-bugs on a '1% of checkout requests take 5s' symptom.
expected_behavior: Engineer writes down ≥ 3 hypotheses BEFORE making any observation: e.g. 'Stripe API slow for some payment methods' / 'Inventory check contention on hot row' / 'Fraud-check service slow for specific user cohort'
For each hypothesis, the engineer predicts the distinguishing observation (cheapest first: Stripe response times by payment method)
First observation: Stripe response times don't correlate → H1 falsified
Second observation: end-to-end trace shows 4.8s in inventory check → H2 confirmed
Localize within inventory: which endpoint? Trace shows /inventory/limited (added 2 weeks ago)
Reduce: minimum input is 'item with stock_count=1'; /inventory/limited takes 4.8s for stock_count=1 but <100ms for stock_count>=2 — root cause is the new FOR UPDATE on a hot row
Fix: replace FOR UPDATE with non-blocking read; add test asserting limited-stock checkout latency < 1s under load
Diagnosis paragraph written (symptom / cause / distinguishing observations / why obvious didn't pan out / fix / guard)
Total observations: 3 (Stripe SQL, end-to-end trace, intra-inventory trace) — converged in < 2 hours

behavioral_evals.scenario: Engineer skips writing down hypotheses and just starts 'trying things'.
expected_behavior: Skill explicitly flags this anti-pattern: 'Observation-driven diagnosis requires a hypothesis set BEFORE observing'
Skill demands: 'Write down 3 hypotheses (specific + testable), predict the distinguishing observation for each, then make the smallest observation'

If engineer insists 'I have one strong hypothesis': skill flags single-hypothesis debugging as anti-pattern — 'without alternatives, you have no distinguishing observation; you can only confirm your existing belief, not eliminate alternatives'
Skill offers: 'If you can write down 3 hypotheses in 5 minutes, you save 30+ minutes of random debugging'

notes: Source adapted from mattpocock/skills diagnosing-bugs. Core skill because hard bugs are a guaranteed part of every project. Pairs with debugging-and-error-recovery (the protocol) by adding the diagnostic discipline inside localize+reduce. The two together form the full debugging cycle: debugging owns the sequence, diagnosing-bugs owns the 'which observation next' question. Verified-level (2026-07-24): positive_keywords assert each appears in description body. Anti-pattern call-outs cover: single-hypothesis debugging, random trying, premature fixing, log-as-diagnosis.
